{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}Ōbe 1-13-17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|44.6|N|135|25|08.5|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおべじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018275 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県川西市小戸1丁目13-17', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小戸神社 (川西市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11460785}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōbe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:小戸神社鳥居 DRtcPk9UIAA5TB6.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Ōbe 1-13-17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|44.6|N|135|25|08.5|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおべじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018275 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県川西市小戸1丁目13-17', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小戸神社 (川西市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11460785}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōbe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:小戸神社鳥居 DRtcPk9UIAA5TB6.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Ōbe 1-13-17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|44.6|N|135|25|08.5|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Ōbe 1-13-17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|44.6|N|135|25|08.5|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおべじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018275 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県川西市小戸1丁目13-17', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小戸神社 (川西市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11460785}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōbe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:小戸神社鳥居 DRtcPk9UIAA5TB6.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Ōbe 1-13-17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|44.6|N|135|25|08.5|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964|1=October 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ōbe Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ōbe Shrine''' (おおべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōbe, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}' it is read "Obe," but today it is called "Ōbe." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Settsu meisho zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}' it says: "Located in Ōbe village. Appears in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki (Register of Deities)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.' It is the ubusuna deity of Ōbe, Sakaine, Kobana and others." It was revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} over the west bank area of the {{ill|lt=Inagawa River|Q11571867|WD=Q11571867|1=Ina River}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}), Yasaka Shrine and Hosokawa-hime Shrine were merged. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}}), a new {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} was built. * In {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}), it was repairedSadatoshi Yoshii, "Ōbe Shrine" ("Shikinaisha Survey Report", Vol. 5, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Precinct shrines === * Fujimori Shrine - Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Ōkami|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Ōkami}} * Kashima Shrine - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} ファイル:小戸神社拝殿 DRtcRVMUIAEZ1Vi.jpg|Haiden ファイル:小戸神社覆殿 DRtcSccUQAEQ0eZ.jpg|Cover hall ファイル:稲荷神社・鹿嶋神社 DRtce4eVwAAypg9.jpg|Inari Shrine and Kashima Shrine === Cultural properties === [[File:Ohbejinja02.jpg|150px|thumb|Great camphor tree (Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument)]] * Great camphor tree, designated {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated March 22, Shōwa 41) - root circumference approx. 12 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, height approx. 30 m, estimated age 500 years. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station|Q3969857|WD=Q3969857|1=Kawanishi-Noseguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305029.html Ōbe Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōbe Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]
